truth_rule: Source code proves only what the inspected revision implements. Community videos prove terminology and demonstrated workflows only in the shown environment. Neither proves private ExtremeCraft parity.

useful_for: modern TNT and falling-block motion order
explosion ray traversal and entity impulse
software_sources.boundary: The repository explicitly warns that it is still buggy and in active development. Treat it as implementation evidence and a research corpus, not a drop-in authority for Sakura or ExtremeCraft.

useful_for: ROM bit encoding for known cannon definitions
software_sources.boundary: CannonLib does not generate a 384 OSRB, reconstruct redstone, solve EC160 architecture, or emulate private server plugins.

useful_for: cannon build and testing workflow context
software_sources.boundary: Utility tooling is not a cannon solver and is not evidence for archetype mechanics.

rejected_or_universalized_claims.reason: No inspected primary source established one universal ratio across archetypes, exposures, versions and target mechanics.

rejected_or_universalized_claims.claim: current Sakura 26.1.2 definitely has the Moonrise early-fuse bug
rejected_or_universalized_claims.reason: A historical Folia/Moonrise issue is not proof of the current pinned runtime or private ExtremeCraft behavior. Use the reference-physics fingerprint test.

rejected_or_universalized_claims.claim: CannonLib can design a complete 384 OSRB
rejected_or_universalized_claims.reason: The inspected library solves narrower trajectory, basket, ROM and bounce problems.
Observed 384-labeled systems contain substantially different total dispenser counts. Treat 384 as a community payload/stack label until the exact design is decoded.
